title: Engine Oil Service / Maintenance Interval Reset
description: Reset oil life or maintenance interval counters after oil and filter replacement.
physical_solution: Reset the oil life or service interval counter after completing the oil and filter change.

technical_solution.elm327_feasibility: Partial — many vehicles allow cluster-button reset without scanner. Scan-tool based resets may need enhanced UDS functions.
preconditions: Oil and filter service physically completed
Ignition ON engine OFF (or per OEM path)

procedure_steps.description: Confirm oil and filter replacement complete. Record mileage and oil spec.
Enter instrument cluster or BCM extended session: $10 03
Write oil life reset or execute reset routine: $2E [OIL_LIFE_DID] or $31 01 [RESET_ROUTINE_ID]
Cycle ignition. Confirm maintenance indicator shows 100% or reset state.
Check for secondary intervals (inspection/tire rotation) that may need separate reset.

oem_notes.Toyota/Lexus: Cluster menu/button reset common. Scan path available on newer platforms.
oem_notes.Honda/Acura: Maintenance Minder reset via MID. Hold SELECT button until confirmation.
oem_notes.Nissan/Infiniti: Multiple reminder channels (oil/filter/tire). Reset each applicable item.
oem_notes.Ford/Lincoln: Oil life monitor via steering wheel menu. Scan reset after IPC/PCM service.
oem_notes.GM: Driver information center reset. Ensure percentage returns to 100%.
oem_notes.BMW/MINI: CBS tracks oil, brakes, microfilter separately. Reset only the correct item.
oem_notes.VW/Audi: Fixed/flexible interval coding affects reset values. Verify market configuration.
oem_notes.Stellantis: Cluster reset available on most models. SGW doesn't typically block this procedure.
oem_notes.Hyundai/Kia: Cluster-based reset on most models. GDS path for module replacement scenarios.
